name: The Attorney Group, Professional Law CORP El Grupo De Abogados
description_short: Multi-location California law firm specializing in bankruptcy (Chapter 7, 13, 11) and related legal services including family law, criminal defense, and personal injury with 80+ years combined attorney experience.
description_long:
The Attorney Group, Professional Law Corp is a law firm operating over 30 locations throughout California, positioning itself as a full-service legal practice. The firm was established to provide bankruptcy and general legal services to consumers facing financial and legal challenges. Their stated mission is to deliver the best results through experienced, focused, and driven legal counsel. The firm emphasizes their combined 80+ years of attorney and paralegal experience across their team.

The Attorney Group offers services across multiple legal practice areas including bankruptcy filing (Chapters 7, 13, and 11), family law matters (child custody, child support, restraining orders), criminal defense (DUI, traffic tickets, warrant recalls, expungements), personal injury litigation, business litigation, lemon law, worker's compensation, tax resolution, civil litigation, and trademark services. Their website explicitly markets bankruptcy services in both English and Spanish ('Abogados de Bancarrota'), indicating bilingual service availability.

The firm differentiates itself through geographic accessibility with 30+ California locations, emphasis on local court knowledge gained from handling thousands of cases, and a stated client-focused approach from initial consultation through case completion. They offer free initial consultations and advertise a single phone number (888-754-9877) for scheduling. The firm markets itself to Spanish-speaking consumers, suggesting cultural and linguistic accessibility.

As a law firm, The Attorney Group represents a traditional service provider model rather than a fintech or lending platform. Consumers should note that legal fees, payment structures, and specific case outcomes are not detailed on the website. While the firm lists extensive practice areas, the concentration of marketing appears weighted toward bankruptcy services. Potential clients should verify licensing status, request fee schedules upfront, and understand that legal representation quality can vary significantly by individual attorney.
address: 122 E 7th St Unit 802, Los Angeles, CA 90014

company_info.headquarters: 122 E 7th St Unit 802, Los Angeles, CA 90014

pros: 30+ California office locations provide geographic accessibility and local court knowledge
Advertises 80+ combined years of attorney and paralegal experience
Offers free initial consultations via single toll-free number (888-754-9877)
Bilingual services available (English and Spanish language marketing)
Handles all three bankruptcy chapter types (7, 13, and 11)
Multi-practice area firm allows potential for consolidated legal representation
States client-focused service model with goal to 'make you feel at ease'
cons: Website provides no information on attorney licensing, Bar status, or individual credentials
No fee schedule, payment plans, or pricing transparency disclosed
Vague claim of '80+ years combined experience' does not specify average attorney experience or specialization depth
No case results, success rates, or client testimonials provided to verify quality
Extensive practice area list (15+ areas) raises questions about specialization depth versus generalist approach
best_for: California residents needing bankruptcy filing assistance (Chapter 7 or Chapter 13) seeking in-person consultation
Spanish-speaking consumers requiring legal services with bilingual capability
Individuals needing multiple legal services (bankruptcy + family law or criminal defense) from single firm
diagnosis: The Attorney Group is best for California consumers facing bankruptcy filing (Chapters 7 or 13) who value in-person local representation and may prefer bilingual services. Primary caveat: The website lacks attorney credential verification, fee transparency, and case outcome data—consumers must independently verify licensing, request detailed fee information before engagement, and discuss specialization level given the firm's extensive practice area list.
services: Family law and child custody representation
